meaning: adj.可代替的,可替换的,可取代的
examples: It's a simple term that encapsulates the fact that relationships at work can reasonably be non-intimate, inconsequential, unimportant and even, dare I say it, disposable or substitutable.
Subtype must be substitutable for their base types.
Derived types must be completely substitutable for their base types.
The idea that natural assets are substitutable makes some environmentalists (including some contributors to the report) nervous.
At the extremes to an economist everything is substitutable.
